differential_diagnoses: Occult infection (e.g., miliary tuberculosis or disseminated fungal infection)
next_steps: Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with cytogenetics and flow cytometry
Computed tomography of the chest with contrast to evaluate for pulmonary malignancy
Serum ferritin, soluble CD25 (sIL-2 receptor), and NK cell activity to evaluate for HLH
Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the spine for structural evaluation and potential biopsy site identification
Repeat peripheral blood smear review by an expert hematopathologist
Supportive care with blood product transfusions as needed based on clinical status
Comprehensive malignancy workup including tumor markers and body imaging

differential_diagnoses: Metastatic carcinoma with marrow infiltration (myelophthisis), likely from occult primary (lung or pancreas)
Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH), secondary to occult malignancy
Myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) with excess blasts or secondary myelofibrosis
Acute leukemia (e.g., acute myeloid leukemia with marrow failure)
Disseminated occult infection (e.g., miliary tuberculosis or fungal)
Systemic mastocytosis with associated hematologic neoplasm
next_steps: Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and immunohistochemistry for epithelial markers
CT of the chest with intravenous contrast to characterize lung nodules
Measurement of serum ADAMTS13 activity to confirm exclusion of TTP
Calculation of H-score for potential HLH using soluble IL-2 receptor (sCD25) and ferritin
Endoscopic ultrasound (EUS) with fine-needle aspiration of the pancreatic tail/lesion
Systemic supportive care, including blood product support and management of acute pancreatitis
Expert hematopathologist review of bone marrow aspirate and biopsy sections
